steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence uses present-tense verbs throughout (“are,” “travel,” “pack”) to describe the Sherpas’ recurring activities. The blank is the second verb in a compound predicate joined by “and,” so it must match the present tense established by “pack.”

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "get": Simple present tense, consistent with “pack” and the other present-tense verbs. ✓
  • "had gotten": Past perfect tense — signals an action completed before another past event, contradicting the present-tense context. ✗
  • "got": Simple past tense, inconsistent with the present-tense verbs used throughout the sentence. ✗
  • "were getting": Past progressive tense, inconsistent with the present-tense context. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
